claude-sonnet-4-6.coherence: Turn 1 is spatially grounded with the guard in the main_hall, but the narrative describes him peering out into a "night street beyond" through a door, which implies movement to or interaction with the entrance/exterior without a committed move, creating mild ambiguity; Turn 2 correctly places characters in the main_hall.

claude-sonnet-4-6.engagement: The prose has genuine atmospheric texture — the scrape of the stool, the spilled ale, the unnerving stillness — and both turns advance the scene with multiple beats, though the unexplained hooded figure in Turn 2 creates a character grounding problem that undermines immersion.

This, combined with the ambiguous door interaction in Turn 1, prevents a score above 3 despite the prose quality being genuinely above average.
